message: feat(wayland): fp16 framebuffers on NVIDIA
patch:
commit 313963abdbea0e583bdb60d0bdb707016cf048ec
Author: Thomas Müller <git@tom94.net>
Date:   Tue Jun 9 10:19:48 2026 +0200

    feat(wayland): fp16 framebuffers on NVIDIA
    
    This relies on a slight hack within GLFW where the EGL opaque extension
    is disabled on wayland in order to obtain a supported fp16 framebuffer
    format. Since tev renders its framebuffer with an alpha of 1 always,
    this is no big deal and will display correctly.
---
 dependencies/nanogui | 2 +-
 src/MultiGraph.cpp   | 4 +++-
 2 files changed, 4 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)

diff --git a/dependencies/nanogui b/dependencies/nanogui

@@ -1 +1 @@
-Subproject commit 8b52b3f110ebe27c94a41e94619eae5d7f054219

diff --git a/src/MultiGraph.cpp b/src/MultiGraph.cpp
index cd2210a..b07b666 100644
--- a/src/MultiGraph.cpp
+++ b/src/MultiGraph.cpp
@@ -61,7 +61,9 @@ void MultiGraph::draw(NVGcontext* ctx) {
         nvgSave(ctx);
 
         // Additive blending
-        nvgGlobalCompositeBlendFunc(ctx, NVGblendFactor::NVG_SRC_ALPHA, NVGblendFactor::NVG_ONE);
+        nvgGlobalCompositeBlendFuncSeparate(
+            ctx, NVGblendFactor::NVG_SRC_ALPHA, NVGblendFactor::NVG_ONE, NVGblendFactor::NVG_ZERO, NVGblendFactor::NVG_ONE
+        );
 
         size_t nBins = mValues.size() / mNChannels;

author: Thomas Müller
message: feat(png): write sRGB chunk on save
patch:
commit 4f37143ad7feff9e9224868053b01c5c3a947606
Author: Thomas Müller <git@tom94.net>
Date:   Wed May 27 07:40:22 2026 +0200

    feat(png): write sRGB chunk on save
    
    While the spec recommends also writing gAMA and cHRM chunks as fallback
    for incompatible decoders, it's technically wrong to describe an
    sRGB-encoded image with a 2.2 gamma curve, so we refrain from writing
    those chunks. https://www.w3.org/TR/png-3/#srgb-standard-colour-space
---
 src/imageio/PngImageSaver.cpp | 2 ++
 1 file changed, 2 insertions(+)

diff --git a/src/imageio/PngImageSaver.cpp b/src/imageio/PngImageSaver.cpp
index b94d035..eaed4f2 100644
--- a/src/imageio/PngImageSaver.cpp
+++ b/src/imageio/PngImageSaver.cpp
@@ -78,6 +78,8 @@ Task<void> PngImageSaver::save(ostream& oStream, const fs::path&, span<const uin
         png, info, imageSize.x(), imageSize.y(), 8, colorType, PNG_INTERLACE_NONE, PNG_COMPRESSION_TYPE_DEFAULT, PNG_FILTER_TYPE_DEFAULT
     );
 
+    png_set_sRGB(png, info, 3 /* absolute colorimetric */);
+
     png_write_info(png, info);
 
     const auto stride = (size_t)imageSize.x() * nChannels;

patch:
commit d97a533c924659aa51c4439580fefd19b8b11ea2
Author: Thomas Müller <git@tom94.net>
Date:   Tue Feb 17 14:05:25 2026 +0100

    feat(readme): compactify formats further
---
 README.md | 4 +---
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+), 3 deletions(-)

diff --git a/README.md b/README.md
index 6c69e23..63cbc77 100644
--- a/README.md
+++ b/README.md
@@ -131,16 +131,14 @@ $ cpack --config build/CPackConfig.cmake
 
 - __EXR__ (via [OpenEXR](https://github.com/AcademySoftwareFoundation/openexr))
 - __JPEG XL__ (including gain maps; via [libjxl](https://github.com/libjxl/libjxl))
-    - Supports both lossy and lossless compression with full HDR capabilities (natively and with gain maps)
 - __JPEG__ (including gain maps, e.g. HDR pictures from Android; via [libjpeg-turbo](https://github.com/libjpeg-turbo/libjpeg-turbo))
 - __JPEG 2000__ (via [openjpeg](https://github.com/uclouvain/openjpeg))
 - __PNG__, APNG (via [libpng](https://github.com/pnggroup/libpng))
 - __PFM__, PAM, PNM, PBM, PGM, PPM (generally: see [NetPBM](https://netpbm.sourceforge.net/) and [PFM](https://www.pauldebevec.com/Research/HDR/PFM/))
 - __QOI__ (via [qoi](https://github.com/phoboslab/qoi))
 - __DDS__ (via [DirectXTex](https://github.com/microsoft/DirectXTex); Windows only)
-    - Supports BC1-BC7 compressed formats.
 - __WEBP__ (via [libwebp](https://chromium.googlesource.com/webm/libwebp))
-- __TIFF__, DNG (via [libtiff](https://gitlab.com/libtiff/libtiff); only some DNG files supported)
+- __TIFF__, __DNG__ (via [libtiff](https://gitlab.com/libtiff/libtiff))
 - __AVIF__ (including gain maps, e.g. HDR pictures from Android; via [aom](https://aomedia.googlesource.com/aom)+[libheif](https://github.com/strukturag/libheif))
 - __HEIC__ (including gain maps, e.g. HDR pictures from iPhones; via [libde265](https://github.com/strukturag/libde265)+[libheif](https://github.com/strukturag/libheif); disabled in binary release. You must build __tev__ yourself with the `TEV_SUPPORT_HEIC` CMake option. Check patent laws in your jurisdiction before enabling this feature.)
 - __BMP__, ICO, CUR (including color profiles)

message: feat(heif): format detection & mini header support
patch:
commit ed7032c7cb41b58444852241f82129b0ae6867c8
Author: Thomas Müller <git@tom94.net>
Date:   Mon Feb 16 08:19:24 2026 +0100

    feat(heif): format detection & mini header support
---
 dependencies/CMakeLists.txt     |  1 +
 src/imageio/HeifImageLoader.cpp | 55 ++++++++++++++++++++++++++---------------
 2 files changed, 36 insertions(+), 20 deletions(-)

diff --git a/dependencies/CMakeLists.txt b/dependencies/CMakeLists.txt
index 5517292..dbcd5d0 100644
--- a/dependencies/CMakeLists.txt
+++ b/dependencies/CMakeLists.txt
@@ -283,6 +283,7 @@ if (TEV_USE_LIBHEIF)
     set(WITH_LIBSHARPYUV OFF CACHE BOOL " " FORCE)
 
     set(ENABLE_PLUGIN_LOADING OFF CACHE BOOL " " FORCE)
+    set(ENABLE_EXPERIMENTAL_MINI_FORMAT ON CACHE BOOL " " FORCE)
 
     add_subdirectory(libheif EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL)
     target_include_directories(heif INTERFACE
diff --git a/src/imageio/HeifImageLoader.cpp b/src/imageio/HeifImageLoader.cpp
index 2a87aca..f40001c 100644
--- a/src/imageio/HeifImageLoader.cpp
+++ b/src/imageio/HeifImageLoader.cpp
@@ -51,31 +51,46 @@ Task<vector<ImageData>> HeifImageLoader::load(
         throw FormatNotSupported{"File is too short to be an HEIF image."};
     }
 
-    const auto mimeTypeParts = split(heif_get_file_mime_type(header, 12), "/");
-    if (mimeTypeParts.size() != 2) {
-        throw FormatNotSupported{"Could not determine HEIF mime type."};
+    if (header[4] != 'f' || header[5] != 't' || header[6] != 'y' || header[7] != 'p') {
+        throw FormatNotSupported{"Invalid HEIF file: missing 'ftyp' box."};
     }
 
-    const auto format = mimeTypeParts.back();
-    unordered_set<string_view> supportedFormats = {
-        // HEIF with HEVC compression
-        "heic",
-        "heic-sequence",
-        // Generic HEIF format, compression not specified
-        "heif",
-        "heif-sequence",
-        // HEIF with AV1 compression
-        "avif",
-        "avif-sequence",
-        // HEIC with JPEG 2000 compression
-        "hej2k",
-        "j2is", // j2 image sequence
+    const heif_brand2 brand = heif_read_main_brand(header, 12);
+
+    unordered_set<heif_brand2> supportedFormats = {
+        // HEIC
+        heif_brand2_heic,
+        heif_brand2_heix,
+        heif_brand2_heim,
+        heif_brand2_heis,
+        // HEIF
+        heif_brand2_mif1,
+        heif_brand2_mif2,
+        heif_brand2_mif3,
+        heif_brand2_miaf,
+        // HEIC sequence
+        heif_brand2_hevc,
+        heif_brand2_hevx,
+        heif_brand2_hevm,
+        heif_brand2_hevs,
+        // HEIF sequence
+        heif_brand2_msf1,
+        // AVIF
+        heif_brand2_avif,
+        // AVIF sequence
+        heif_brand2_avis,
+        // JPEG 2000
+        heif_brand2_j2ki,
+        // JPEG 2000 sequence
+        heif_brand2_j2is,
         // JPEG
-        "jpeg",
+        heif_brand2_jpeg,
+        // JPEG sequence
+        heif_brand2_jpgs,
     };
 
-    if (supportedFormats.find(format) == supportedFormats.end()) {
-        throw FormatNotSupported{fmt::format("HEIF format '{}' is not supported.", format)};
+    if (supportedFormats.find(brand) == supportedFormats.end()) {
+        throw FormatNotSupported{fmt::format("HEIF format {:08X} is not supported.", brand)};
     }
 
     iStream.seekg(0, ios_base::end);

author: Thomas Müller
message: feat(heif,jpeg2000): support jpeg2000/jpeg encoded HEIF
patch:
commit 6ffce9be992928a831250179ea0415ec7e85dd16
Author: Thomas Müller <git@tom94.net>
Date:   Sun Feb 15 13:10:31 2026 +0100

    feat(heif,jpeg2000): support jpeg2000/jpeg encoded HEIF
---
 .gitmodules                     |  4 ++++
 CMakeLists.txt                  |  2 +-
 dependencies/CMakeLists.txt     | 20 ++++++++++++++++++++
 dependencies/libheif            |  2 +-
 dependencies/openjpeg           |  1 +
 src/imageio/HeifImageLoader.cpp | 22 +++++++++++++++++++---
 6 files changed, 46 insertions(+), 5 deletions(-)

diff --git a/.gitmodules b/.gitmodules
index c5bf25a..8b4c058 100644
--- a/.gitmodules
+++ b/.gitmodules
@@ -106,3 +106,7 @@
 	path = dependencies/concurrentqueue
 	url = https://github.com/cameron314/concurrentqueue
 	shallow = true
+[submodule "dependencies/openjpeg"]
+	path = dependencies/openjpeg
+	url = https://github.com/tom94/openjpeg
+	shallow = true
diff --git a/CMakeLists.txt b/CMakeLists.txt
index 9a8314e..78bb3be 100644
--- a/CMakeLists.txt
+++ b/CMakeLists.txt
@@ -268,7 +268,7 @@ endif()
 
 target_compile_definitions(tev PRIVATE ${TEV_DEFINITIONS})
 
-set(TEV_LIBS clip exif_static jpeg-turbo libraw::libraw_r nanogui OpenEXR::OpenEXR png_static tiff webpdemux XMPCore)
+set(TEV_LIBS clip exif_static jpeg-turbo libraw::libraw_r nanogui OpenEXR::OpenEXR openjp2 png_static tiff webpdemux XMPCore)
 if (MSVC)
     list(APPEND TEV_LIBS DirectXTex wsock32 ws2_32)
 endif()
diff --git a/dependencies/CMakeLists.txt b/dependencies/CMakeLists.txt
index e3fcb7d..5517292 100644
--- a/dependencies/CMakeLists.txt
+++ b/dependencies/CMakeLists.txt
@@ -149,6 +149,20 @@ set_target_properties(jpeg-turbo PROPERTIES
 add_library(JPEG::JPEG ALIAS jpeg-turbo)
 set(JPEG_FOUND TRUE CACHE BOOL " " FORCE)
 
+# Compile OpenJPEG for JPEG 2000 support
+set(BUILD_SHARED_LIBS OFF CACHE BOOL " " FORCE)
+set(BUILD_CODEC OFF CACHE BOOL " " FORCE)
+add_subdirectory(openjpeg EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL)
+
+# The following variables ensure that find_package can find OpenJPEG
+set(OpenJPEG_INCLUDE_DIRS
+    ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/openjpeg/src/lib/openjp2
+    ${CMAKE_CURRENT_BINARY_DIR}/openjpeg/src/lib/openjp2
+)
+
+set(OpenJPEG_FOUND TRUE CACHE BOOL " " FORCE)
+set(OPENJPEG_FOUND TRUE CACHE BOOL " " FORCE)
+
 # Compile aom (dependency of libheif, which follows)
 if (TEV_SUPPORT_AVIF)
     set(AOM_TARGET_NAME aom)
@@ -261,12 +275,15 @@ if (TEV_USE_LIBHEIF)
     set(WITH_AOM_DECODER ${TEV_SUPPORT_AVIF} CACHE BOOL " " FORCE)
 
     set(WITH_JPEG_DECODER ON CACHE BOOL " " FORCE)
+    set(WITH_OpenJPEG_DECODER ON CACHE BOOL " " FORCE)
 
     # libsharpyuv is not needed for us, because we're not interested in converting from RGB to YUV. tev is primarily an image viewer that
     # goes from YUV to RGB upon loading images, where libsharpyuv does nothing. See https://www.ctrl.blog/entry/webp-sharp-yuv.html for more
     # info.
     set(WITH_LIBSHARPYUV OFF CACHE BOOL " " FORCE)
 
+    set(ENABLE_PLUGIN_LOADING OFF CACHE BOOL " " FORCE)
+
     add_subdirectory(libheif EXCLUDE_FROM_ALL)
     target_include_directories(heif INTERFACE
         ${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/libheif/libheif/api
@@ -276,6 +293,9 @@ if (TEV_USE_LIBHEIF)
     add_dependencies(heif ${JPEGTURBO_TARGET_NAME})
     target_include_directories(heif PRIVATE "${JPEG_INCLUDE_DIRS}")
 
+    target_include_directories(heif PRIVATE "${OpenJPEG_INCLUDE_DIRS}")
+    target_link_libraries(heif PRIVATE openjp2)
+
     if (TEV_SUPPORT_AVIF)
         add_dependencies(heif ${AOM_TARGET_NAME})
         target_include_directories(heif PRIVATE ${AOM_BINARY_DIR})
diff --git a/dependencies/libheif b/dependencies/libheif
index f8b5f07..0c773c6 160000
--- a/dependencies/libheif
+++ b/dependencies/libheif
@@ -1 +1 @@
-Subproject commit f8b5f07e662a71b98734117a3a5ad518c7a63839
+Subproject commit 0c773c659def78b7ac35afba74322f3c528cb5c7
diff --git a/dependencies/openjpeg b/dependencies/openjpeg
new file mode 160000
index 0000000..bf06e71
--- /dev/null
+++ b/dependencies/openjpeg
@@ -0,0 +1 @@
+Subproject commit bf06e714b5036ea7d501056ab4c8287edd402760
diff --git a/src/imageio/HeifImageLoader.cpp b/src/imageio/HeifImageLoader.cpp
index f2c6d24..2a87aca 100644
--- a/src/imageio/HeifImageLoader.cpp
+++ b/src/imageio/HeifImageLoader.cpp
@@ -32,6 +32,7 @@
 #include <libheif/heif_sequences.h>
 
 #include <optional>
+#include <unordered_set>
 
 using namespace nanogui;
 using namespace std;
@@ -55,10 +56,25 @@ Task<vector<ImageData>> HeifImageLoader::load(
         throw FormatNotSupported{"Could not determine HEIF mime type."};
     }
 
-    // We support heic, heif and avif formats, with and without sequence.
     const auto format = mimeTypeParts.back();
-    if (format != "heic" && format != "heic-sequence" && format != "heif" && format != "heif-sequence" && format != "avif" &&
-        format != "avif-sequence") {
+    unordered_set<string_view> supportedFormats = {
+        // HEIF with HEVC compression
+        "heic",
+        "heic-sequence",
+        // Generic HEIF format, compression not specified
+        "heif",
+        "heif-sequence",
+        // HEIF with AV1 compression
+        "avif",
+        "avif-sequence",
+        // HEIC with JPEG 2000 compression
+        "hej2k",
+        "j2is", // j2 image sequence
+        // JPEG
+        "jpeg",
+    };
+
+    if (supportedFormats.find(format) == supportedFormats.end()) {
         throw FormatNotSupported{fmt::format("HEIF format '{}' is not supported.", format)};
     }

diff --git a/README.md b/README.md
index 99aa79d..769f1f3 100644
--- a/README.md
+++ b/README.md
@@ -142,7 +142,8 @@ $ cpack --config build/CPackConfig.cmake
 - __TIFF__, DNG (via [libtiff](https://gitlab.com/libtiff/libtiff); only some DNG files supported)
 - __AVIF__ (including gain maps, e.g. HDR pictures from Android; via [aom](https://aomedia.googlesource.com/aom)+[libheif](https://github.com/strukturag/libheif))
 - __HEIC__ (including gain maps, e.g. HDR pictures from iPhones; via [libde265](https://github.com/strukturag/libde265)+[libheif](https://github.com/strukturag/libheif); disabled in binary release. You must build __tev__ yourself with the `TEV_SUPPORT_HEIC` CMake option. Check patent laws in your jurisdiction before enabling this feature.)
-- __HDR__, BMP, GIF, PIC, PSD, TGA (via [stb_image](https://github.com/wjakob/nanovg/blob/master/src/stb_image.h))
+- __BMP__, ICO, CUR (including color profiles)
+- __HDR__, GIF, PIC, PSD, TGA (via [stb_image](https://github.com/wjakob/nanovg/blob/master/src/stb_image.h))
 - __RAW__ and various camera-specific RAW formats (via [LibRaw](https://github.com/LibRaw/LibRaw))

message: fix(windows): dynamic linkage to openmp via libraw
patch:
commit 1227b5b2f89caa149cc7904900e0bb7cd8a965d7
Author: Thomas Müller <git@tom94.net>
Date:   Fri Feb 13 13:08:49 2026 +0100

    fix(windows): dynamic linkage to openmp via libraw
    
    Previously, a dynamic openmp dependency snuck into tev via libraw,
    making tev fail to start (dll not found) on systems without MSVC
    redistributable. Not good! This commit hard-disables openmp in libraw to
    prevent this issue.
---
 dependencies/CMakeLists.txt | 1 +
 1 file changed, 1 insertion(+)

diff --git a/dependencies/CMakeLists.txt b/dependencies/CMakeLists.txt
index 646a3c1..e3fcb7d 100644
--- a/dependencies/CMakeLists.txt
+++ b/dependencies/CMakeLists.txt
@@ -371,6 +371,7 @@ target_compile_definitions(clip PRIVATE -DHAVE_PNG_H)
 
 # Compile libraw
 set(LIBRAW_PATH "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/LibRaw" CACHE PATH " " FORCE)
+set(ENABLE_OPENMP OFF CACHE BOOL " " FORCE)
 set(LCMS2_INCLUDE_DIR "${CMAKE_CURRENT_SOURCE_DIR}/Little-CMS/include" CACHE PATH " " FORCE)
 set(LCMS2_LIBRARIES lcms2-tev CACHE STRING " " FORCE)
 add_subdirectory(LibRaw-cmake)

    feat(metainfo): add back TEV_VALIDATE_METAINFO option
---
 CMakeLists.txt | 19 ++++++++++++++-----
 1 file changed, 14 insertions(+), 5 deletions(-)

diff --git a/CMakeLists.txt b/CMakeLists.txt
index 7fbff2d..d11b216 100644
--- a/CMakeLists.txt
+++ b/CMakeLists.txt
@@ -54,6 +54,7 @@ option(TEV_SUPPORT_HEIC "Support loading HEIC images" OFF)
 option(TEV_SUPPORT_JXL "Support loading/saving JPEG XL images" ON)
 
 option(TEV_CREATE_METAINFO "Auto-generate metainfo for flatpak release" OFF)
+option(TEV_VALIDATE_METAINFO "Validate generated metainfo for flatpak release" OFF)
 
 set(TEV_USE_LIBHEIF OFF)
 if (TEV_SUPPORT_AVIF OR TEV_SUPPORT_HEIC)
@@ -366,14 +367,22 @@ else()
 
         configure_file(resources/linux/tev.metainfo.xml.in resources/linux/tev.metainfo.xml)
         install(FILES ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/resources/linux/tev.metainfo.xml DESTINATION "${CMAKE_INSTALL_DATAROOTDIR}/metainfo" RENAME "${TEV_LINUX_APP_ID}.metainfo.xml")
+    endif()
+
+    if (TEV_VALIDATE_METAINFO)
+        if (NOT TEV_CREATE_METAINFO)
+            message(FATAL_ERROR "TEV_VALIDATE_METAINFO is set but TEV_CREATE_METAINFO is not set. Cannot validate non-existing metainfo.")
+        endif()
 
         find_program(APPSTREAMCLI_EXECUTABLE appstreamcli)
-        if (APPSTREAMCLI_EXECUTABLE)
-            add_custom_command(
-                TARGET tev POST_BUILD
-                COMMAND ${APPSTREAMCLI_EXECUTABLE} validate ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/resources/linux/tev.metainfo.xml
-            )
+        if (NOT APPSTREAMCLI_EXECUTABLE)
+            message(FATAL_ERROR "appstreamcli not found -- cannot validate metainfo.")
         endif()
+
+        add_custom_command(
+            TARGET tev POST_BUILD
+            COMMAND ${APPSTREAMCLI_EXECUTABLE} validate ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/resources/linux/tev.metainfo.xml
+        )
     endif()
 
     set(ICON_RESOLUTIONS "16;24;32;48;64;96;128;256;512")

diff --git a/CMakeLists.txt b/CMakeLists.txt

 option(TEV_SUPPORT_HEIC "Support loading HEIC images" OFF)
 option(TEV_SUPPORT_JXL "Support loading/saving JPEG XL images" ON)
 
-option(TEV_VALIDATE_METAINFO "Validate metainfo after successful build" OFF)
+option(TEV_CREATE_METAINFO "Auto-generate metainfo for flatpak release" OFF)
 
 set(TEV_USE_LIBHEIF OFF)
 if (TEV_SUPPORT_AVIF OR TEV_SUPPORT_HEIC)
@@ -351,15 +351,29 @@ else()
     configure_file(resources/linux/tev.desktop resources/linux/tev.desktop)
     install(FILES ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/resources/linux/tev.desktop DESTINATION "${CMAKE_INSTALL_DATAROOTDIR}/applications" RENAME "${TEV_LINUX_APP_ID}.desktop")
 
-    configure_file(resources/linux/tev.metainfo.xml.in resources/linux/tev.metainfo.xml)
-    install(FILES ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/resources/linux/tev.metainfo.xml DESTINATION "${CMAKE_INSTALL_DATAROOTDIR}/metainfo" RENAME "${TEV_LINUX_APP_ID}.metainfo.xml")

-            TARGET tev POST_BUILD
-            COMMAND ${APPSTREAMCLI_EXECUTABLE} validate ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/resources/linux/tev.metainfo.xml
+    if (TEV_CREATE_METAINFO)
+        execute_process(
+            COMMAND git for-each-ref "refs/tags/v*.*" --shell --sort=-creatordate --format [=[
+                tag=%(refname:short)
+                version="${tag#v}"
+                printf '        <release version="%s" date="%s">\n            <url type="details">https://github.com/Tom94/tev/releases/tag/%s</url>\n        </release>\n' "$version" %(creatordate:short) "$tag"

+            COMMAND sh
+            WORKING_DIRECTORY "${PROJECT_SOURCE_DIR}"
+            OUTPUT_STRIP_TRAILING_WHITESPACE
+            OUTPUT_VARIABLE TEV_METAINFO_RELEASES
         )
+
+        configure_file(resources/linux/tev.metainfo.xml.in resources/linux/tev.metainfo.xml)
+        install(FILES ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/resources/linux/tev.metainfo.xml DESTINATION "${CMAKE_INSTALL_DATAROOTDIR}/metainfo" RENAME "${TEV_LINUX_APP_ID}.metainfo.xml")
+
+        find_program(APPSTREAMCLI_EXECUTABLE appstreamcli)
+        if (APPSTREAMCLI_EXECUTABLE)
+            add_custom_command(
+                TARGET tev POST_BUILD
+                COMMAND ${APPSTREAMCLI_EXECUTABLE} validate ${CMAKE_BINARY_DIR}/resources/linux/tev.metainfo.xml
+            )
+        endif()
     endif()
 
     set(ICON_RESOLUTIONS "16;24;32;48;64;96;128;256;512")

message: fix(wayland): consistent scroll scaling
patch:
commit 3d5c560b7f8687d55a9b8c188db893f49442e8df
Author: Thomas Müller <git@tom94.net>
Date:   Wed Oct 22 20:09:47 2025 +0200

    fix(wayland): consistent scroll scaling
    
    Previously, GLFW under Wayland would often count scroll wheel detents as
    1.5 units worth of scrolling vs. other OS's and X11's counting of 1.
    
    This commit updates tev's fork of GLFW to use more precise scroll units
    (via a newer version of the Wayland protocol) if available.
